zh.lifeSummary: 當Geoffrey de Mandeville於大約1036年出生在法國諾門迪時，他的父親William de Mandeville31歲，母親Mrs. William Maneville31歲。他於大約1053年與Adeliza Balts在英國英格蘭奧可福爾利可特結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1144年9月16日在英國素福爾可米爾登哈爾享年109歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭米德萊可維斯特明斯特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Geoffrey de Mandeville于大约1036年出生在法国諾門迪时，他的父亲William de Mandeville31岁，母亲Mrs. William Maneville31岁。他于大约1053年与Adeliza Balts在英国英格兰奧可福爾利可特结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1144年9月16日在英国素福爾可米爾登哈爾享年109岁，埋葬在英国英格兰米德萊可維斯特明斯特。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Mandeville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Armand, Gabrielle, Camille, Emile, Fernand, Fernande, Gaston, Jean-Paul, Lucien, Michel.

French: habitational name from Mandeville, the name of two places in Normandy, derived from Latin magna villa ‘large estate’, or a variant of Manneville, a habitational name from Manneville, the name of several places in Normandy (see 2 below). Compare Montville and Monville .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Manneville (Seine-Maritime, Calvados), Manneville-sur-Risle (Eure), or Magneville (Manche).

variants.name: Mansfield
meanings: English: habitational name from a place so named in Nottinghamshire. The placename means ‘open land by the river Maun’, from the river name Maun (which may take its name from a hill named with Old English mamme ‘teat’) + Old English feld ‘open country’.
Irish (Waterford): when not an importation of 1, this is an altered form of the Norman name de Manville (see Mandeville 3).
Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) Mansfeld, a habitational name from a place so called in Saxony.

variants.name: Manderville
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Mandeville  .

variants.name: Manwell
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Mandeville  .

variants.name: Manville
meanings: English (Sussex and Lancashire): variant of   Mandeville  .
Altered form of   Mainville   1, a surname of Swiss French origin (see   Miville  ).
French: variant of Menville, a habitational name from Menville in Haute-Garonne, and also of Mainville 2.

variants.name: Mundell
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin): variant of   Mandeville  .
Americanized form of German Mündel (or some other similar, like-sounding surname): nickname for someone with a prominent mouth, from Middle High German munt, mündelīn ‘(little) mouth’.

variants.name: Monville
meanings: Altered form of French   Mandeville  .
Walloon: habitational name from Monville in Namur province in Wallonia (Belgium), of the same etymology as in 3 below.
In some cases possibly also French: habitational name from Montville in Seine-Maritime or Monville in Calvados, both named with Old French mont ‘mountain, hill’ + ville ‘settlement’.

variants.name: Mandal
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Chandi, Sanjay, Sankar, Somnath, Aditi, Aditya, Ajit, Amala, Amitabha, Anil, Anirudh, Aparna.
Indian (northern states): from Sanskrit maṇḍala, a word that has several meanings, including ‘sphere’ and ‘halo’.
English (Cumbria; of Norman origin): variant of   Mandeville  .

variants.name: Montville
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Vernice.
Altered form of French   Mandeville  .
French: variant of   Monville  . This surname is very rare in France, found mainly in Creuse.

variants.name: Manfull
meanings: English (Midlands): probably a variant of   Mandeville  . Compare   Manville  .

nameMeanings.name: Geoffrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic (Frankish and Lombard) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It was in regular use among the counts of Anjou, ancestors of the English royal house of Plantagenet, who were descended from Geoffrey Plantagenet, Count of Anjou ( 1113–51 ). It was a particularly popular name in England and France in the later Middle Ages; notable bearers in England include the poet Geoffrey Chaucer ( c.1340–1400 ) and in Wales the chronicler Geoffrey of Monmouth ( d. 1155 ). The original form and meaning of the name is disputed. According to one theory, it is merely a variant of Godfrey ; others derive the first part from the Germanic word gawia ‘territory’, walah ‘stranger’, or gisil ‘pledge’. Medieval forms can be found to support all these theories, and it is possible that several names have fallen together, or that the name was subjected to reanalysis by folk etymology at an early date.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Geoffrey de Mandeville was born about 1036, in Normandy, France, his father, William de Mandeville, was 31 and his mother, Mrs. William Maneville, was 31. He married Adeliza Balts about 1053, in Rycote, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 16 September 1144, in Mildenhall, Suffolk, England, at the age of 109, and was buried in Westminster, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Geoffrey de Mandeville est né environ 1036, à Normandie, France, son père, William de Mandeville, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Mrs. William Maneville, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Adeliza Balts environ 1053, à Rycote, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 16 septembre 1144, à Mildenhall, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 109 ans, et a été enterré à Westminster, Middlesex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Geoffrey de Mandeville um 1036 in Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater William de Mandeville 31 und seine Mutter Mrs. William Maneville 31. Er hat um 1053 in Rycote, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom Adeliza Balts geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 16. September 1144 im Alter von 109 Jahren in Mildenhall, Suffolk, England und wurde in Westminster, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Geoffrey de Mandeville è nato il circa 1036 nel Normandie, Francia, il suo padre, William de Mandeville, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Mrs. William Maneville, aveva 31 anni. Lui sposato Adeliza Balts circa 1053, nel Rycote, Oxfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 16 settembre 1144, nel Mildenhall, Suffolk, England, all'età di 109 anni, e fu sepolto a Westminster, Middlesex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de Mandevilleが約1036年にフランスノーマンディで生まれた時，父William de Mandevilleは31歳，母Mrs. William Manevilleは31歳であった。約1053年にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアリコトでAdeliza Baltsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1144年9月16日にイギリススフォルクミルデンハルで109歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスミッドレセックスウエストミンスターにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de Mandeville(이)가 대략 1036년 프랑스 노먼디에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de Mandeville(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. William Maneville(은)는 31세였다. 그는 대략 1053년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 리컷에서 Adeliza Balts(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1144년 9월 16일 영국 수폴크 미데홀에서 109세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스 웨스트민스터에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Geoffrey de Mandeville nasceu aproximadamente 1036, em Normandie, França, seu pai, William de Mandeville, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. William Maneville, tinha 31 anos. Ele casou-se com Adeliza Balts aproximadamente 1053, em Rycote, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 16 de setembro de 1144, em Mildenhall, Suffolk, Inglaterra, com 109 anos, e foi sepultado em Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Geoffrey de Mandeville родился около 1036 г. в местности норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени William de Mandeville было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Mrs. William Maneville, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Adeliza Balts около 1053 г. в местности рикот, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 16 сентября 1144 г. в местности маилденолл, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 109 лет и был похоронен в местности вестминстир, миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания.

Se casó con Adeliza Balts aproximadamente 1053, en Rycote, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 16 de septiembre de 1144, en Mildenhall, Suffolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 109 años, y fue sepultado en Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
